rag_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, place implementation-level bindings like assignments inside component implementation annexes, not type annexes.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers, data types, or property references that are not present in the input AADL model.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers within the same annex to avoid type errors.
Ensure that guarantee expressions match the actual types of their operands.
Avoid unsafe range comparisons on Boolean or opaque data ports; use exact Boolean expressions when appropriate.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not add architecture elements or assumptions that are not present in the AADL model.
Do not generate guarantees with unresolved identifiers or mismatched types.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about empty packages
